behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI/report wiring. Rename classification can differ based on actual git diff behavior (e.g., similarity thresholds and flags), which a mock often simplifies and would miss. This requires a real repository, real file operations, and real CLI execution. Higher levels are disqualified because no AI/ML output is involved and there is no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it with content preserved, run the CLI against real repo state, and assert report shows exactly 1 renamed and 0 added/0 deleted for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling depends on real git subprocess output and actual binary file treatment. In-process fakes can hide parser/encoding/subprocess edge cases that appear only with real binary blobs and real git output. Higher levels are disqualified because this is not model-output dependent and has no agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit baseline, add/modify a real binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in the correct count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The critical failure occurs at the real git repository/subprocess boundary: unborn HEAD and git exit/error semantics. Mocking git responses can miss real command behavior and stderr/exit-code handling. Higher levels are disqualified because no AI dependency or multi-step agent workflow exists.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty git repo with no commits, execute the CLI, assert zero exit status and an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real commits, index/worktree state, and git query behavior. A mocked history provider could mirror expected values and mask contract mismatches with git CLI output. Higher levels are disqualified because correctness does not depend on AI/ML generation/classification.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run CLI, and assert reported totals per category exactly match independently computed ground truth from real git history commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are defined by real git index/worktree behavior. The failure appears when interacting with actual repository state transitions and git commands; in-process mocks can miss these subtleties. Higher levels are disqualified because this is deterministic git/CLI behavior with no model decision-making or agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI in both states, and assert structured stdout is identical for both runs.
